Question: Quality bar and validation criteria for the simulation harness
Resolution Notes: Full simulation harness committed for MVP, but the threshold for 'good enough' is not yet defined. Harness catches obvious failures (arc never ends, killer never identified, safety triggers wrong) but does not predict whether real groups enjoy a session. Need to define: pass/fail criteria for synthetic runs, when to ship Nightcap based on harness results vs real playtests.
Where Resolved: First few weeks of harness usage; tune thresholds based on what synthetic runs reveal.

Question: Verify specific GPT-OSS-Safeguard 20B pricing and integration on Groq before MVP build
Resolution Notes: Groq pricing for GPT-OSS 20B was visible (input $0.075/M cached) but full output rate and integration via LiteLLM need verification. NVIDIA acquired Groq in Dec 2025; build for portability via LiteLLM abstraction.
Where Resolved: Pre-MVP build kickoff (Chat 7 or first implementation session in Claude Code).

Question: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL specific configuration at MVP (instance class, storage, backup retention)
Resolution Notes: Decided on Cloud SQL PostgreSQL but not the specific tier. db-f1-micro is the smallest; db-g1-small or shared-core may be needed for prod traffic. Storage starts at 10GB. Free GCP credits ($300) cover ~20 months of minimum config. Specific config TBD when actual session volume materializes.
Where Resolved: Implementation kickoff in Claude Code; revisit at first month of real traffic.

Question: Full telemetry schema for Tier 2 fine-tuning viability (Chat 6 deliverable, RESOLVED)
Resolution Notes: Designed in Chat 6a (May 7, 2026). MVP minimum signals: arc beat engagement duration, pacing intervention triggers/outcomes, knowledge state constraint activations, session completion status, replay intent. Tier 2 signals (designed, partial implementation at MVP): generation_logs table, decision_logs table, quality signals, player engagement depth, knowledge graph evolution snapshots, safety classifier outcomes, character behavior consistency, group dynamics signals. Embedding-ready schema. Full schema in Chat 6b handoff prompt.
Where Resolved: Chat 6a, May 7, 2026. Schema documented in Chat 6b handoff.

Question: Which voice synthesis service to integrate for future games that want voice (ElevenLabs vs OpenAI vs Replica vs other)
Resolution Notes: Out of scope for Nightcap MVP (web app, text-based). Becomes relevant for monster RPG or couch co-op if voice acting is desired. Architecture supports voice via voice_hint in content event presentation_hints. Game layer integrates with whichever TTS service it chooses.
Where Resolved: Per-game decision when voice is in scope. H2 monster RPG or H2/H3 couch co-op.

Question: When to add Layer 4 (post-generation safety) to the safety pipeline
Resolution Notes: Layer 4 deferred at MVP per Chat 6a Round 3 commitment, with watchpoint to add if real sessions surface output problems. Trigger: any single output incident or pattern of borderline output that the main LLM produces.
Where Resolved: First N (N=10-20) real-user playtests post-MVP. Review safety logs and adjust.

Question: When in H2/H3 to begin building the visual arc builder
Resolution Notes: Schema designed for visual-builder-readiness at MVP. Implementation timing depends on external developer adoption signal (closed beta opens) and design bandwidth. Watch for: external developers asking for non-code authoring, designer/storyteller hires.
Where Resolved: H2 review when external developer beta is being scoped, or earlier if a strategic design hire joins.

Question: When does external developer access to the platform open (Horizon 2 closed beta scope and selection)
Resolution Notes: PRD says external developer beta is H2 priority gated by Nightcap proving the platform internally. Selection process not yet defined: invite-only, application-based, or specific partner-targeted. Documentation maturity is also a gating factor.
Where Resolved: H2 planning chat when proof signals are within 1-2 quarters of hitting H2 entry.

Question: Open source self-hosting break-even (Chat 6 deliverable, RESOLVED)
Resolution Notes: Calculated in Chat 6a (May 7, 2026). At managed API rates (Anthropic Sonnet 4.6 + Groq GPT-OSS-Safeguard) per-session cost is ~$0.20 with prompt caching. Self-hosting Llama 3.3 70B-class on a single A100 80GB at $2/hr does not break even with managed pricing until ~25,000-50,000 sessions/month, depending on utilization. Conclusion: stay on managed APIs through H2. Architecture supports switching via LiteLLM abstraction (routing-table change, not rewrite). Tier 2 fine-tuning trigger is ~25K sessions/month.
Where Resolved: Chat 6a, May 7, 2026.

Question: What platform capabilities should the third game (couch co-op) validate beyond Nightcap and monster RPG
Resolution Notes: Deferred per Chat 6a Option B (couch co-op design). Architecture validation in Chat 6b uses placeholder capabilities (shared screen rendering, local input, low-latency same-room mechanics, mixed cooperative-competitive structure). Genre and specific mechanics decision belongs in a future dedicated chat.
Where Resolved: Chat 8.5 or equivalent dedicated to couch co-op design, post Nightcap MVP.

Question: At what session volume does open source self-hosting become cheaper than managed API pricing?
Resolution Notes: Chat 6 must calculate break-even given: projected token consumption per session at each player count, GPU instance costs for open source inference, and managed API pricing at projected volume tiers. This determines when and whether to trigger Tier 2 infrastructure.
Where Resolved: Chat 6 (Technical Architecture)

Question: What are the H2-to-H3 transition signal thresholds?

Question: What platform capability gaps should the third game close, and which game closes them?

Remaining candidates: asynchronous play, very short sessions (under 10 minutes), solo adaptive experience, real-time competitive mechanics.

Question: Story structure templates as first-class platform objects: the current arc schema includes arc_structure as a metadata string field but the engine does not use it to scaffold beat definitions or pacing defaults. Should story structure templates (Dan Harmon, Hero's Journey, etc.) be first-class platform objects that pre-populate beat scaffolding for arc authors, or is this purely an authoring-layer concern deferred to the no-code arc builder in Horizon 2? Resolution must clarify whether arc_structure remains decorative metadata at MVP or whether the engine needs to understand it functionally.
Resolution Notes: Resolved in Chat 7 Story Bible. Story Circle is now a first-class platform object. arc_structure: "story_circle" scaffolds 8 pre-populated beat containers with story_circle_step, structural_function, dramatic_purpose, emotional_target, information_goal, tension_target, and character_emphasis fields. The engine uses structural intent as a functional input for generation context, pacing calibration, and ensemble coordination. arc_structure is no longer decorative metadata. arc_structure value changed from dan_harmon to story_circle. Technical Architecture updated to v1.2 with full schema changes. Story structure templates are first-class platform objects at MVP, not deferred to Horizon 2.
Where Resolved: docs/story-bibles/nightcap-murder-mystery.md | docs/architecture/ | docs/product/decisions-log.csv
